name: Skill Challenge: Beat 24 — The Djupá Approach

text.content:
Skill Challenge: Beat 24 — The Djupá Approach

Act: Act 3 | Difficulty: Hard (DC 15)

Successes Needed: 6 | Failures Allowed: 3

Overview

Beat 24 — The Djupá Approach

The party uses the Djupá valley to approach Hrafnborg from the north, racing to reach the city before the siege ring closes the last gap. Dreyfus's dying information gives them twelve hours.

Difficulty: Hard — this is a timed montage. Urgency is the defining quality. The valley narrows as it approaches Hrafnborg. The siege ring has scouts. The party is being hunted as they move.

Refugee Variant: If refugees travel with the party, Round 2 challenges involving Presence become more critical — managing 40+ people through a closing siege ring.

Running the Skill Challenge

Each character takes a turn attempting one skill check. On their turn, a player describes how they're helping the party and rolls the appropriate skill against DC 15. A success adds to the group's total; a failure adds to the failure count. If the party reaches 6 successes before 3 failures, they succeed overall.

Suggested Skills:

  • Athletics
  • Acrobatics or Stealth
  • Arcana or Investigation
  • Perception, Survival, or Insight
  • Persuasion, Intimidation, or Deception

Players may also suggest creative skill uses — reward good roleplaying with advantage or a lower DC.

Complications

Round 1: The Djupá Valley

The party enters the valley from the north. The river runs roughly south-southwest toward Hrafnborg, cutting through a narrow passage.

ChallengeCharacteristicDescription
Navigate the valley floor in falling lightReasonThe valley is navigable but darkness is coming fast. Finding the path by memory and observation as the light dies.
Scout the valley walls for siege scoutsAgility / IntuitionThe undead siege includes scouts on higher ground. The party needs to spot these before being spotted.
Move quickly without alerting the siege ringAgilitySpeed matters — twelve hours. Moving fast but staying silent on steep riverbed terrain.
Keep pace and manage cold exposureMightThe Djupá valley runs cold. Wind is funneled down the riverbed. Cold exposure and exhaustion are real threats on a forced march.
Gather intelligence about the siege ring's positionIntuitionHow far has the siege ring extended into the valley? Is there still a gap? Reading signs: smoke position, sound, tracks in snow.
Make tactical decisions about the routePresence / ReasonThe group needs to stay coordinated and committed to a route even as circumstances change. Leadership in motion.

Later Rounds

Round 2: The Final Approach

The siege ring's eastern advantage is beginning to include the Djupá valley's mouth. The party must thread through before it closes.

ChallengeCharacteristicDescription
Time the undead patrol patternReason / IntuitionThe undead move in timed rotations. Spotting the pattern and identifying the gap — the window where the party can move unseen.
Navigate the valley mouth without triggering alarmAgilityGetting through the narrowing gap without triggering a general alarm. One wrong move and the entire eastern flank converges.
Reach Hrafnborg's north gatePresence / MightThe gate is still open, barely. Defenders are watching. The party must reach it before the siege ring tightens or fight through.
Manage refugees' pace in the final stretchPresenceIf refugees travel with the party — getting 40+ people through the siege ring gap without panic.
Spot opportunities to moveIntuitionThe undead patterns have gaps — moments where the siege ring is thin. Spotting these windows.
Commit to the final pushPresence / MightThe last mile is a sprint. The gap is closing. The gate is close. Everything depends on the final push without hesitation.

Outcomes

Interlude — Hrafnborg Becomes Visible: As the party crests a rise, Hrafnborg becomes visible. South walls of dark stone, towers stark against grey sky. Undead in timed rotations. Defenders on the walls. The Ever-Ember's gold pulse visible through the great hall window. The siege ring is closing — the eastern flank beginning to wheel northward. Time is running out.

Hard Success (6+ successes): Enter the city undetected. No alarm triggered. The defenders don't even need to fight. The gap closes after the party passes through.

Success (4–5 successes): Enter with minor cost. One alarm triggered — brief skirmish at the gate, but nothing organized. Inside before the siege ring consolidates.

Failure (2–3 successes): Arrive pursued. Fighting through the gate — defenders must hold the opening. Short but sharp (3 rounds of combat at the gate-barrier).

Hard Failure (0–1 successes): The gap closes before reaching the gate. Must fight through the siege ring itself — Hard combat encounter, multiple waves. Eventually break through, but significant cost.
